betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Modular Computers: Cases & Core Components§f

§aConstruct a customizable, high-performance modular computer to automate your base!§f
Unlike traditional single-block machines, OpenComputers requires you to assemble custom computer systems by choosing cases and inserting core components.

§6Computer Case Tiers§f:
§b• Tier 1 Case (Green)§f: Entry-level. Has limited component slots (2 card slots, 2 container slots, 2 expansion slots) and supports lower tier components.
§b• Tier 2 Case (Yellow)§f: Mid-grade. Medium slots and supports mid-tier components.
§b• Tier 3 Case (Cyan)§f: High-end. Maximum slots (3 card slots, 3 container slots, 3 expansion slots) and supports high-tier components.

§6Core internal components§f:
§b• Central Processing Unit (CPU)§f: Drives the computer's speed and execution. Available in §bTier 1§f, §bTier 2§f, and §bTier 3§f. High-tier CPUs allow more components to run simultaneously.
§b• Memory (RAM)§f: Needed to load programs and libraries. Ranges from §bTier 1 (128 KB)§f up to §bTier 3.5 (1.5 MB or higher)§f. If your computer crashes with "out of memory" errors, you need more RAM!
§b• Graphics Card (GPU)§f: Controls screen rendering capabilities:
  §e• Tier 1 GPU§f: 50×16 resolution, 1-bit monochrome display.
  §e• Tier 2 GPU§f: 80×25 resolution, 4-bit color palette (16 colors).
  §e• Tier 3 GPU§f: 160×50 resolution, 8-bit color depth (256 colors).

§9Assembly Guide§f:
Shift + Right-click a placed §bComputer Case§f to open its component GUI. Drag your §bCPU§f, §bRAM§f, §bGPU§f, and other cards into their respective slots, then press the power button to boot up the system.
